NKJV.1: Then He said to the disciples, “It is impossible that no offenses should come, but woe to him through whom they do come!
NKJV.2: It would be better for him if a millstone were hung around his neck, and he were thrown into the sea, than that he should offend one of these little ones.
NKJV.3: Take heed to yourselves.  If your brother sins against you, rebuke him; and if he repents, forgive him.
NKJV.4: And if he sins against you seven times in a day, and seven times in a day returns to you, saying, ‘I repent,’ you shall forgive him.”
NKJV.5: And the apostles said to the Lord, “Increase our faith.”
NKJV.6: So the Lord said, “If you have faith as a mustard seed, you can say to this mulberry tree, ‘Be pulled up by the roots and be planted in the sea,’ and it would obey you.
NKJV.7: And which of you, having a servant plowing or tending sheep, will say to him when he has come in from the field, ‘Come at once and sit down to eat’?
NKJV.8: But will he not rather say to him, ‘Prepare something for my supper, and gird yourself  and serve me till I have eaten and drunk, and afterward you will eat and drink’?
NKJV.9: Does he thank that servant because he did the things that were commanded him? I think not.
NKJV.10: So likewise you, when you have done all those things which you are commanded, say, ‘We are  unprofitable servants. We have done what was our duty to do.’ ”
NKJV.11: Now it happened as He went to Jerusalem that He passed through the midst of Samaria and Galilee.
NKJV.12: Then as He entered a certain village, there met Him ten men who were lepers, who stood afar off.
NKJV.13: And they lifted up their voices and said, “Jesus, Master, have mercy on us!”
NKJV.14: So when He saw them, He said to them, “Go, show yourselves to the priests.” And so it was that as they went, they were cleansed.
NKJV.15: And one of them, when he saw that he was healed, returned, and with a loud voice glorified God,
NKJV.16: and fell down on his face at His feet, giving Him thanks. And he was a Samaritan.
NKJV.17: So Jesus answered and said, “Were there not ten cleansed? But where are the nine?
NKJV.18: Were there not any found who returned to give glory to God except this foreigner?”
NKJV.19: And He said to him, “Arise, go your way. Your faith has made you well.”
NKJV.20: Now when He was asked by the Pharisees when the kingdom of God would come, He answered them and said, “The kingdom of God does not come with observation;
NKJV.21: nor will they say, ‘See here!’ or ‘See there!’ For indeed,  the kingdom of God is within you.”
NKJV.22: Then He said to the disciples, “The days will come when you will desire to see one of the days of the Son of Man, and you will not see it.
NKJV.23: And they will say to you, ‘Look here!’ or ‘Look there!’ Do not go after them or follow them.
NKJV.24: For as the lightning that flashes out of one part under heaven shines to the other part under heaven, so also the Son of Man will be in His day.
NKJV.25: But first He must suffer many things and be  rejected by this generation.
NKJV.26: And as it  was in the  days of  Noah, so it will be also in the days of the Son of Man:
NKJV.27: They ate, they drank, they married wives, they were given in marriage, until the  day that Noah entered the ark, and the flood came and  destroyed them all.
NKJV.28: Likewise as it was also in the days of Lot: They ate, they drank, they bought, they sold, they planted, they built;
NKJV.29: but on the day that Lot went out of Sodom it rained fire and brimstone from heaven and destroyed them all.
NKJV.30: Even so will it be in the day when the Son of Man  is revealed.
NKJV.31: “In that day, he who is on the housetop, and his goods are in the house, let him not come down to take them away. And likewise the one who is in the field, let him not turn back.
NKJV.33: Whoever seeks to save his life will lose it, and whoever loses his life will preserve it.
NKJV.34: I tell you, in that night there will be two men in one bed: the one will be taken and the other will be left.
NKJV.35: Two women will be grinding together: the one will be taken and the other left.
NKJV.36: Two men will be in the field: the one will be taken and the other left.”
NKJV.37: And they answered and said to Him, “Where, Lord?” So He said to them, “Wherever the body is, there the eagles will be gathered together.”

ESV.1: And he said to his disciples,  “Temptations to sin  are sure to come, but woe to the one through whom they come!
ESV.2: It would be better for him if a millstone were hung around his neck and he were cast into the sea than that he should cause one of these little ones to sin.
ESV.3: Pay attention to yourselves! If your brother sins, rebuke him, and if he repents, forgive him,
ESV.4: and if he sins against you seven times in the day, and turns to you seven times, saying, ‘I repent,’ you must forgive him.”
ESV.5: The apostles said to the Lord, “Increase our faith!”
ESV.6: And the Lord said,  “If you had faith like a grain of mustard seed, you could say to this mulberry tree, ‘Be uprooted and planted in the sea,’ and it would obey you.
ESV.7: “Will any one of you who has a servant  plowing or keeping sheep say to him when he has come in from the field, ‘Come at once and recline at table’?
ESV.8: Will he not rather say to him, ‘Prepare supper for me, and dress properly,  and serve me while I eat and drink, and afterward you will eat and drink’?
ESV.9: Does he thank the servant because he did what was commanded?
ESV.10: So you also, when you have done all that you were commanded, say, ‘We are unworthy servants;  we have only done what was our duty.’”
ESV.11: On the way to Jerusalem he was passing along between Samaria and Galilee.
ESV.12: And as he entered a village, he was met by ten lepers,  who stood at a distance
ESV.13: and lifted up their voices, saying, “Jesus, Master, have mercy on us.”
ESV.14: When he saw them he said to them, “Go and show yourselves to the priests.” And as they went they were cleansed.
ESV.15: Then one of them, when he saw that he was healed, turned back, praising God with a loud voice;
ESV.16: and he fell on his face at Jesus' feet, giving him thanks. Now he was a Samaritan.
ESV.17: Then Jesus answered, “Were not ten cleansed? Where are the nine?
ESV.18: Was no one found to return and give praise to God except this foreigner?”
ESV.19: And he said to him, “Rise and go your way; your faith has made you well.”
ESV.20: Being asked by the Pharisees when the kingdom of God would come, he answered them, “The kingdom of God is not coming in ways that can be observed,
ESV.21: nor will they say, ‘Look, here it is!’ or ‘There!’ for behold, the kingdom of God is in the midst of you.”
ESV.22: And he said to the disciples,  “The days are coming when you will desire to see one of the days of the Son of Man, and you will not see it.
ESV.23: And they will say to you, ‘Look, there!’ or ‘Look, here!’ Do not go out or follow them.
ESV.24: For as the lightning flashes and lights up the sky from one side to the other, so will the Son of Man be in his day.
ESV.25: But first he must suffer many things and be rejected by this generation.
ESV.26: Just as it was in the days of Noah, so will it be in the days of the Son of Man.
ESV.27: They were eating and drinking and marrying and being given in marriage, until the day when Noah entered the ark, and the flood came and destroyed them all.
ESV.28: Likewise, just as it was in the days of Lot — they were eating and drinking, buying and selling, planting and building,
ESV.29: but on the day when Lot went out from Sodom, fire and sulfur rained from heaven and destroyed them all —
ESV.30: so will it be on the day when the Son of Man is revealed.
ESV.31: On that day, let the one who is on the housetop, with his goods in the house, not come down to take them away, and likewise let the one who is in the field not turn back.
ESV.33: Whoever seeks to preserve his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life will keep it.
ESV.34: I tell you, in that night there will be two in one bed. One will be taken and the other left.
ESV.35: There will be two women grinding together. One will be taken and the other left.”
ESV.37: And they said to him, “Where, Lord?” He said to them,  “Where the corpse  is, there the vultures  will gather.”

NASB.1: He said to His disciples, 'It is inevitable that stumbling blocks come, but woe to him through whom they come!
NASB.2: 'It would be better for him if a millstone were hung around his neck and he were thrown into the sea, than that he would cause one of these little ones to stumble.
NASB.3: 'Be on your guard! If your brother sins, rebuke him; and if he repents, forgive him.
NASB.4: 'And if he sins against you seven times a day, and returns to you seven times, saying, 'I repent,' forgive him.'
NASB.5: The apostles said to the Lord, 'Increase our faith!'
NASB.6: And the Lord said, 'If you had faith like a mustard seed, you would say to this mulberry tree, 'Be uprooted and be planted in the sea'; and it would obey you.
NASB.7: 'Which of you, having a slave plowing or tending sheep, will say to him when he has come in from the field, 'Come immediately and sit down to eat'?
NASB.8: 'But will he not say to him, 'Prepare something for me to eat, and [properly] clothe yourself and serve me while I eat and drink; and afterward you may eat and drink '?
NASB.9: 'He does not thank the slave because he did the things which were commanded, does he?
NASB.10: 'So you too, when you do all the things which are commanded you, say, 'We are unworthy slaves; we have done [only] that which we ought to have done.''
NASB.11: While He was on the way to Jerusalem, He was passing between Samaria and Galilee.
NASB.12: As He entered a village, ten leprous men who stood at a distance met Him;
NASB.13: and they raised their voices, saying, 'Jesus, Master, have mercy on us!'
NASB.14: When He saw them, He said to them, 'Go and show yourselves to the priests.' And as they were going, they were cleansed.
NASB.15: Now one of them, when he saw that he had been healed, turned back, glorifying God with a loud voice,
NASB.16: and he fell on his face at His feet, giving thanks to Him. And he was a Samaritan.
NASB.17: Then Jesus answered and said, 'Were there not ten cleansed? But the nine-- where are they?
NASB.18: 'Was no one found who returned to give glory to God, except this foreigner?'
NASB.19: And He said to him, 'Stand up and go; your faith has made you well.'
NASB.20: Now having been questioned by the Pharisees as to when the kingdom of God was coming, He answered them and said, 'The kingdom of God is not coming with signs to be observed;
NASB.21: nor will they say, 'Look, here [it is]!' or, 'There [it is]!' For behold, the kingdom of God is in your midst.'
NASB.22: And He said to the disciples, 'The days will come when you will long to see one of the days of the Son of Man, and you will not see it.
NASB.23: 'They will say to you, 'Look there! Look here!' Do not go away, and do not run after [them].
NASB.24: 'For just like the lightning, when it flashes out of one part of the sky, shines to the other part of the sky, so will the Son of Man be in His day.
NASB.25: 'But first He must suffer many things and be rejected by this generation.
NASB.26: 'And just as it happened in the days of Noah, so it will be also in the days of the Son of Man:
NASB.27: they were eating, they were drinking, they were marrying, they were being given in marriage, until the day that Noah entered the ark, and the flood came and destroyed them all.
NASB.28: 'It was the same as happened in the days of Lot: they were eating, they were drinking, they were buying, they were selling, they were planting, they were building;
NASB.29: but on the day that Lot went out from Sodom it rained fire and brimstone from heaven and destroyed them all.
NASB.30: 'It will be just the same on the day that the Son of Man is revealed.
NASB.31: 'On that day, the one who is on the housetop and whose goods are in the house must not go down to take them out; and likewise the one who is in the field must not turn back.
NASB.33: 'Whoever seeks to keep his life will lose it, and whoever loses [his life] will preserve it.
NASB.34: 'I tell you, on that night there will be two in one bed; one will be taken and the other will be left.
NASB.35: 'There will be two women grinding at the same place; one will be taken and the other will be left.
NASB.36: ['Two men will be in the field; one will be taken and the other will be left.]'
NASB.37: And answering they said to Him, 'Where, Lord?' And He said to them, 'Where the body [is], there also the vultures will be gathered.'

NLT.1: One day Jesus said to his disciples, “There will always be temptations to sin, but what sorrow awaits the person who does the tempting!
NLT.2: It would be better to be thrown into the sea with a millstone hung around your neck than to cause one of these little ones to fall into sin.
NLT.3: So watch yourselves! “If another believer sins, rebuke that person; then if there is repentance, forgive.
NLT.4: Even if that person wrongs you seven times a day and each time turns again and asks forgiveness, you must forgive.”
NLT.5: The apostles said to the Lord, “Show us how to increase our faith.”
NLT.6: The Lord answered, “If you had faith even as small as a mustard seed, you could say to this mulberry tree, ‘May you be uprooted and be planted in the sea,’ and it would obey you!
NLT.7: “When a servant comes in from plowing or taking care of sheep, does his master say, ‘Come in and eat with me’?
NLT.8: No, he says, ‘Prepare my meal, put on your apron, and serve me while I eat. Then you can eat later.’
NLT.9: And does the master thank the servant for doing what he was told to do? Of course not.
NLT.10: In the same way, when you obey me you should say, ‘We are unworthy servants who have simply done our duty.’”
NLT.11: As Jesus continued on toward Jerusalem, he reached the border between Galilee and Samaria.
NLT.12: As he entered a village there, ten men with leprosy stood at a distance,
NLT.13: crying out, “Jesus, Master, have mercy on us!”
NLT.14: He looked at them and said, “Go show yourselves to the priests.” And as they went, they were cleansed of their leprosy.
NLT.15: One of them, when he saw that he was healed, came back to Jesus, shouting, “Praise God!”
NLT.16: He fell to the ground at Jesus’ feet, thanking him for what he had done. This man was a Samaritan.
NLT.17: Jesus asked, “Didn’t I heal ten men? Where are the other nine?
NLT.18: Has no one returned to give glory to God except this foreigner?”
NLT.19: And Jesus said to the man, “Stand up and go. Your faith has healed you. ”
NLT.20: One day the Pharisees asked Jesus, “When will the Kingdom of God come?” Jesus replied, “The Kingdom of God can’t be detected by visible signs.
NLT.21: You won’t be able to say, ‘Here it is!’ or ‘It’s over there!’ For the Kingdom of God is already among you. ”
NLT.22: Then he said to his disciples, “The time is coming when you will long to see the day when the Son of Man returns, but you won’t see it.
NLT.23: People will tell you, ‘Look, there is the Son of Man,’ or ‘Here he is,’ but don’t go out and follow them.
NLT.24: For as the lightning flashes and lights up the sky from one end to the other, so it will be on the day when the Son of Man comes.
NLT.25: But first the Son of Man must suffer terribly and be rejected by this generation.
NLT.26: “When the Son of Man returns, it will be like it was in Noah’s day.
NLT.27: In those days, the people enjoyed banquets and parties and weddings right up to the time Noah entered his boat and the flood came and destroyed them all.
NLT.28: “And the world will be as it was in the days of Lot. People went about their daily business — eating and drinking, buying and selling, farming and building —
NLT.29: until the morning Lot left Sodom. Then fire and burning sulfur rained down from heaven and destroyed them all.
NLT.30: Yes, it will be ‘business as usual’ right up to the day when the Son of Man is revealed.
NLT.31: On that day a person out on the deck of a roof must not go down into the house to pack. A person out in the field must not return home.
NLT.32: Remember what happened to Lot’s wife!
NLT.33: If you cling to your life, you will lose it, and if you let your life go, you will save it.
NLT.34: That night two people will be asleep in one bed; one will be taken, the other left.
NLT.35: Two women will be grinding flour together at the mill; one will be taken, the other left. ”
NLT.37: “Where will this happen, Lord?” the disciples asked. Jesus replied, “Just as the gathering of vultures shows there is a carcass nearby, so these signs indicate that the end is near.”

WEB.1: He said to the disciples, “It is impossible that no occasions of stumbling should come, but woe to him through whom they come!
WEB.2: It would be better for him if a millstone were hung around his neck, and he were thrown into the sea, rather than that he should cause one of these little ones to stumble.
WEB.3: Be careful. If your brother sins against you, rebuke him. If he repents, forgive him.
WEB.4: If he sins against you seven times in the day, and seven times returns, saying, ‘I repent,’ you shall forgive him.”
WEB.5: The apostles said to the Lord, “Increase our faith.”
WEB.6: The Lord said, “If you had faith like a grain of mustard seed, you would tell this sycamore tree, ‘Be uprooted, and be planted in the sea,’ and it would obey you.
WEB.7: But who is there among you, having a servant plowing or keeping sheep, that will say, when he comes in from the field, ‘Come immediately and sit down at the table,’
WEB.8: and will not rather tell him, ‘Prepare my supper, clothe yourself properly, and serve me, while I eat and drink. Afterward you shall eat and drink’?
WEB.9: Does he thank that servant because he did the things that were commanded? I think not.
WEB.10: Even so you also, when you have done all the things that are commanded you, say, ‘We are unworthy servants. We have done our duty.’”
WEB.11: As he was on his way to Jerusalem, he was passing along the borders of Samaria and Galilee.
WEB.12: As he entered into a certain village, ten men who were lepers met him, who stood at a distance.
WEB.13: They lifted up their voices, saying, “Jesus, Master, have mercy on us!”
WEB.14: When he saw them, he said to them, “Go and show yourselves to the priests.” As they went, they were cleansed.
WEB.15: One of them, when he saw that he was healed, turned back, glorifying God with a loud voice.
WEB.16: He fell on his face at Jesus’ feet, giving him thanks; and he was a Samaritan.
WEB.17: Jesus answered, “Weren’t the ten cleansed? But where are the nine?
WEB.18: Were there none found who returned to give glory to God, except this stranger?”
WEB.19: Then he said to him, “Get up, and go your way. Your faith has healed you.”
WEB.20: Being asked by the Pharisees when God’s Kingdom would come, he answered them, “God’s Kingdom doesn’t come with observation;
WEB.21: neither will they say, ‘Look, here!’ or, ‘Look, there!’ for behold, God’s Kingdom is within you.”
WEB.22: He said to the disciples, “The days will come, when you will desire to see one of the days of the Son of Man, and you will not see it.
WEB.23: They will tell you, ‘Look, here!’ or ‘Look, there!’ Don’t go away, nor follow after them,
WEB.24: for as the lightning, when it flashes out of the one part under the sky, shines to the other part under the sky; so will the Son of Man be in his day.
WEB.25: But first, he must suffer many things and be rejected by this generation.
WEB.26: As it was in the days of Noah, even so will it be also in the days of the Son of Man.
WEB.27: They ate, they drank, they married, they were given in marriage, until the day that Noah entered into the ship, and the flood came, and destroyed them all.
WEB.28: Likewise, even as it was in the days of Lot: they ate, they drank, they bought, they sold, they planted, they built;
WEB.29: but in the day that Lot went out from Sodom, it rained fire and sulfur from the sky, and destroyed them all.
WEB.30: It will be the same way in the day that the Son of Man is revealed.
WEB.31: In that day, he who will be on the housetop, and his goods in the house, let him not go down to take them away. Let him who is in the field likewise not turn back.
WEB.33: Whoever seeks to save his life loses it, but whoever loses his life preserves it.
WEB.34: I tell you, in that night there will be two people in one bed. The one will be taken, and the other will be left.
WEB.35: There will be two grinding grain together. One will be taken, and the other will be left.”
WEB.37: They, answering, asked him, “Where, Lord?” He said to them, “Where the body is, there will the vultures also be gathered together.”

CSB17.1: He <sup>ⓓ</sup> said to his disciples, “Offenses will certainly come, <sup>[1]</sup>but woe <sup>ⓔ</sup>to the one through whom they come! <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.2: It would be better for him if a millstone were hung around his neck and he were thrown into the sea than for him to cause one of these little ones to stumble. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.3: Be <sup>ⓗ</sup>on your guard. If your brother sins, <sup>[2]</sup>rebuke <sup>ⓘ</sup>him, and if he repents, forgive <sup>ⓙ</sup>him.
CSB17.4: And if he sins against you seven times in a day, and comes back to you seven times, saying, ‘I repent,’ you must forgive him.”
CSB17.5: The <sup>ⓚ</sup> apostles <sup>ⓛ</sup> said to the Lord, “Increase our faith.” <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.6: “If you have faith the size of <sup>[3]</sup> a mustard seed,” <sup>ⓝ</sup> the Lord said, “you can say to this mulberry tree, ‘Be uprooted and planted in the sea,’ and it will obey you.
CSB17.7: “Which one of you having a servant tending sheep or plowing will say to him when he comes in from the field, ‘Come at once and sit down to eat’?
CSB17.8: Instead, will he not tell him, ‘Prepare something for me to eat, get ready, and serve me while I eat and drink; later you can eat and drink’? <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.9: Does he thank that servant because he did what was commanded? <sup>[4]</sup>
CSB17.10: In the same way, when you have done all that you were commanded, you should say, ‘We are worthless <sup>ⓑ</sup>servants; we’ve only done our duty.’”
CSB17.11: While traveling to Jerusalem, <sup>ⓒ</sup> he passed between <sup>[5]</sup> Samaria and Galilee. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.12: As he entered a village, ten men with leprosy <sup>[6]</sup> <sup>ⓔ</sup> met him. They stood at a distance
CSB17.13: and raised their voices, saying, “Jesus, Master, <sup>ⓕ</sup> have mercy on us!”
CSB17.14: When he saw them, he told them, “Go and show yourselves to the priests.” <sup>ⓖ</sup> And while they were going, they were cleansed.
CSB17.15: But one of them, seeing that he was healed, returned and, with a loud voice, gave glory to God. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.16: He fell facedown <sup>ⓘ</sup> at his feet, thanking him. And he was a Samaritan. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.17: Then Jesus said, “Were not ten cleansed? Where are the nine?
CSB17.18: Didn’t any return to give glory to God except this foreigner?”
CSB17.19: And he told him, “Get up and go on your way. Your faith has saved you.” <sup>[7]</sup> <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.20: Being <sup>ⓛ</sup> asked by the Pharisees when the kingdom of God would come, <sup>ⓜ</sup> he answered them, “The kingdom of God is not coming with something observable;
CSB17.21: no one will say, <sup>[8]</sup>‘See here! ’ or ‘There! ’ For you see, the kingdom of God is in your midst.” <sup>[9]</sup>
CSB17.22: Then he told the disciples: “The days are coming <sup>ⓝ</sup>when you will long to see one of the days of the Son of Man, but you won’t see it. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.23: They will say to you, <sup>ⓑ</sup>‘See there! ’ or ‘See here! ’ Don’t follow or run after them. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.24: For as the lightning flashes from horizon to horizon and lights up the sky, so the Son of Man will be in his day. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.25: But first it is necessary that he suffer many things and be rejected by this generation. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.26: “Just as it was in the days of Noah, <sup>ⓕ</sup> so it will be in the days of the Son of Man:
CSB17.27: People went on eating, drinking, marrying and giving in marriage <sup>ⓖ</sup>until the day Noah boarded the ark, <sup>ⓗ</sup>and the flood came and destroyed them all.
CSB17.28: It will be the same as it was in the days of Lot: <sup>ⓘ</sup>People went on eating, drinking, buying, selling, planting, building.
CSB17.29: But on the day Lot left Sodom, fire and sulfur rained from heaven and destroyed <sup>ⓙ</sup>them all.
CSB17.30: It <sup>ⓚ</sup>will be like that on the day the Son of Man is revealed. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.31: On that day, a man on the housetop, whose belongings are in the house, must not come down to get them. Likewise the man who is in the field must not turn back.
CSB17.33: Whoever tries to make his life secure <sup>[10]</sup> <sup>[11]</sup>will lose it, and whoever loses his life will preserve it. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.34: I tell you, on that night <sup>ⓐ</sup>two will be in one bed; one will be taken and the other will be left.
CSB17.35: Two women will be grinding grain together; one will be taken and the other left.” <sup>[12]</sup>
CSB17.37: “Where, Lord?” <sup>ⓑ</sup> they asked him. He said to them, “Where the corpse is, there also the vultures <sup>ⓒ</sup>will be gathered.”

AMP.1: Jesus said to His disciples, "Stumbling blocks [temptations and traps set to lure one to sin] are sure to come, but woe (judgment is coming) to him through whom they come!
AMP.2: It would be better for him if a millstone [as large as one turned by a donkey] were hung around his neck and he were hurled into the sea, than for him to cause one of these little ones to stumble [in sin and lose faith].
AMP.3: Pay attention and always be on guard [looking out for one another]! If your brother sins and disregards God's precepts, solemnly warn him; and if he repents and changes, forgive him.
AMP.4: Even if he sins against you seven times a day, and returns to you seven times and says, 'I repent,' you must forgive him [that is, give up resentment and consider the offense recalled and annulled]."
AMP.5: The apostles said to the Lord, "Increase our faith [our ability to confidently trust in God and in His power]."
AMP.6: And the Lord said, "If you have [confident, abiding] faith in God [even as small] as a mustard seed, you could say to this mulberry tree [which has very strong roots], 'Be pulled up by the roots and be planted in the sea'; and [if the request was in agreement with the will of God] it would have obeyed you.
AMP.7: "Which of you who has a servant plowing or tending sheep will say to him when he comes in from the field, 'Come at once and sit down to eat?'
AMP.8: Will he not instead say to him, 'Prepare something for me to eat, and appropriately clothe yourself [for service] and serve me while I eat and drink; then afterward you may eat and drink?'
AMP.9: He does not thank the servant just because he did what he was ordered to do, does he?
AMP.10: So you too, when you have done everything that was assigned and commanded you, say, 'We are unworthy servants [undeserving of praise or a reward, for we have not gone beyond our obligation]; we have merely done what we ought to do.'"
AMP.11: While Jesus was on the way to Jerusalem, He was passing [along the border] between Samaria and Galilee.
AMP.12: As He entered a village, He was met by ten lepers who stood at a distance;
AMP.13: and they raised their voices and called out, "Jesus, Master, have mercy on us!"
AMP.14: When He saw them, He said to them, "Go and show yourselves to the priests." And as they went, they were [miraculously] healed and made clean.
AMP.15: One of them, when he saw that he was healed, turned back, glorifying and praising and honoring God with a loud voice;
AMP.16: and he lay face downward at Jesus' feet, thanking Him [over and over]. He was a Samaritan.
AMP.17: Then Jesus asked, "Were not ten [of you] cleansed? Where are the [other] nine?
AMP.18: Was there no one found to return and to give thanks and praise to God, except this foreigner?"
AMP.19: Jesus said to him, "Get up and go [on your way]. Your faith [your personal trust in Me and your confidence in God's power] has restored you to health."
AMP.20: Now having been asked by the Pharisees when the kingdom of God would come, He replied, "The kingdom of God is not coming with signs to be observed or with a visible display;
AMP.21: nor will people say, 'Look! Here it is!' or, 'There it is!' For the kingdom of God is among you [because of My presence]."
AMP.22: Then He said to the disciples, "The time will come when you will long to see [even] one of the days of the Son of Man, and you will not see it.
AMP.23: They will say to you, 'Look [the Messiah is] there!' or 'Look [He is] here!' Do not go away [to see Him], and do not run after them.
AMP.24: For just like the lightning, when it flashes out of one part of the sky, gives light to the other part of the sky, so [visible] will the Son of Man be in His day.
AMP.25: But first He must suffer many things and be repudiated and rejected and considered unfit [to be the Messiah] by this [unbelieving] generation.
AMP.26: And just as it was in the days of Noah, so it will be in the time of [the second coming of] the Son of Man:
AMP.27: the people were eating, they were drinking, they were marrying, they were being given in marriage, [they were indifferent to God] until the day that Noah went into the ark, and the flood came and destroyed them all.
AMP.28: It was the same as it was in the days of Lot. People were eating, they were drinking, they were buying, they were selling, they were planting, they were building [carrying on business as usual, without regard for their sins];
AMP.29: but on the [very] day that Lot left Sodom it rained fire and brimstone (burning sulfur) from heaven and destroyed them all.
AMP.30: It will be just the same on the day that the Son of Man is revealed.
AMP.31: On that day, whoever is on the housetop, with his belongings in the house, must not come down [and go inside] to take them out; and likewise whoever is in the field must not turn back.
AMP.32: Remember [what happened to] Lot's wife [when she looked back]!
AMP.33: Whoever seeks to save his life will [eventually] lose it [through death], and whoever loses his life [in this world] will keep it [from the consequences of sin and separation from God].
AMP.34: I tell you, on that night [when Messiah comes again] there will be two [sleeping] in one bed; the one (the non-believer) will be taken [away in judgment] and the other (the believer) will be left.
AMP.35: There will be two women grinding [at the mill] together; the one (the non-believer) will be taken [away in judgment] and the other (the believer) will be left.
AMP.36: ["Two men will be in the field; one will be taken and the other will be left."]
AMP.37: And they asked Him, "Where, Lord?" He answered, "Where the corpse is, there the vultures will be gathered."

MSG.1: He said to his disciples, "Hard trials and temptations are bound to come, but too bad for whoever brings them on!
MSG.2: Better to wear a millstone necklace and take a swim in the deep blue sea than give even one of these dear little ones a hard time!
MSG.3: "Be alert. If you see your friend going wrong, correct him. If he responds, forgive him.
MSG.4: Even if it's personal against you and repeated seven times through the day, and seven times he says, 'I'm sorry, I won't do it again,' forgive him."
MSG.5: The apostles came up and said to the Master, "Give us more faith."
MSG.6: But the Master said, "You don't need more faith. There is no 'more' or 'less' in faith. If you have a bare kernel of faith, say the size of a poppy seed, you could say to this sycamore tree, 'Go jump in the lake,' and it would do it.
MSG.7: "Suppose one of you has a servant who comes in from plowing the field or tending the sheep. Would you take his coat, set the table, and say, 'Sit down and eat'?
MSG.8: Wouldn't you be more likely to say, 'Prepare dinner; change your clothes and wait table for me until I've finished my coffee; then go to the kitchen and have your supper'?
MSG.9: Does the servant get special thanks for doing what's expected of him?
MSG.10: It's the same with you. When you've done everything expected of you, be matter-of-fact and say, 'The work is done. What we were told to do, we did.'"
MSG.11: It happened that as he made his way toward Jerusalem, he crossed over the border between Samaria and Galilee.
MSG.12: As he entered a village, ten men, all lepers, met him. They kept their distance
MSG.13: but raised their voices, calling out, "Jesus, Master, have mercy on us!"
MSG.14: Taking a good look at them, he said, "Go, show yourselves to the priests." They went, and while still on their way, became clean.
MSG.15: One of them, when he realized that he was healed, turned around and came back, shouting his gratitude, glorifying God.
MSG.16: He kneeled at Jesus' feet, so grateful. He couldn't thank him enough—and he was a Samaritan.
MSG.17: Jesus said, "Were not ten healed? Where are the nine?
MSG.18: Can none be found to come back and give glory to God except this outsider?"
MSG.19: Then he said to him, "Get up. On your way. Your faith has healed and saved you."
MSG.20: Jesus, grilled by the Pharisees on when the kingdom of God would come, answered, "The kingdom of God doesn't come by counting the days on the calendar.
MSG.21: Nor when someone says, 'Look here!' or, 'There it is!' And why? Because God's kingdom is already among you."
MSG.22: He went on to say to his disciples, "The days are coming when you are going to be desperately homesick for just a glimpse of one of the days of the Son of Man, and you won't see a thing.
MSG.23: And they'll say to you, 'Look over there!' or, 'Look here!' Don't fall for any of that nonsense.
MSG.24: The arrival of the Son of Man is not something you go out to see. He simply comes. "You know how the whole sky lights up from a single flash of lightning? That's how it will be on the Day of the Son of Man.
MSG.25: But first it's necessary that he suffer many things and be turned down by the people of today.
MSG.26: "The time of the Son of Man will be just like the time of Noah—
MSG.27: everyone carrying on as usual, having a good time right up to the day Noah boarded the ship. They suspected nothing until the flood hit and swept everything away.
MSG.28: "It was the same in the time of Lot—the people carrying on, having a good time, business as usual
MSG.29: right up to the day Lot walked out of Sodom and a firestorm swept down and burned everything to a crisp.
MSG.30: That's how it will be—sudden, total—when the Son of Man is revealed.
MSG.31: "When the Day arrives and you're out working in the yard, don't run into the house to get anything. And if you're out in the field, don't go back and get your coat.
MSG.32: Remember what happened to Lot's wife!
MSG.33: If you grasp and cling to life on your terms, you'll lose it, but if you let that life go, you'll get life on God's terms.
MSG.34: "On that Day, two men will be in the same boat fishing—one taken, the other left.
MSG.35: Two women will be working in the same kitchen—one taken, the other left."
MSG.37: Trying to take all this in, the disciples said, "Master, where?" He told them, "Watch for the circling of the vultures. They'll spot the corpse first. The action will begin around my dead body."

NIV.1:
Sin, Faith, Duty
Jesus said to his disciples: “Things that cause people to sin are bound to come, but woe to that person through whom they come.
NIV.2: It would be better for him to be thrown into the sea with a millstone tied around his neck than for him to cause one of these little ones to sin.
NIV.3:
So watch yourselves.
“If your brother sins, rebuke him, and if he repents, forgive him.
NIV.4: If he sins against you seven times in a day, and seven times comes back to you and says, ‘I repent,’ forgive him.”
NIV.5: The apostles said to the Lord, “Increase our faith!”
NIV.6: He replied, “If you have faith as small as a mustard seed, you can say to this mulberry tree, ‘Be uprooted and planted in the sea,’ and it will obey you.
NIV.7: “Suppose one of you had a servant plowing or looking after the sheep. Would he say to the servant when he comes in from the field, ‘Come along now and sit down to eat’?
NIV.8: Would he not rather say, ‘Prepare my supper, get yourself ready and wait on me while I eat and drink; after that you may eat and drink’?
NIV.9: Would he thank the servant because he did what he was told to do?
NIV.10: So you also, when you have done everything you were told to do, should say, ‘We are unworthy servants; we have only done our duty.’”
NIV.11:
Ten Healed of Leprosy
Now on his way to Jerusalem, Jesus traveled along the border between Samaria and Galilee.
NIV.12: As he was going into a village, ten men who had leprosy met him. They stood at a distance
NIV.13: and called out in a loud voice, “Jesus, Master, have pity on us!”
NIV.14: When he saw them, he said, “Go, show yourselves to the priests.” And as they went, they were cleansed.
NIV.15: One of them, when he saw he was healed, came back, praising God in a loud voice.
NIV.16: He threw himself at Jesus’ feet and thanked him—and he was a Samaritan.
NIV.17: Jesus asked, “Were not all ten cleansed? Where are the other nine?
NIV.18: Was no one found to return and give praise to God except this foreigner?”
NIV.19: Then he said to him, “Rise and go; your faith has made you well.”
NIV.20:
The Coming of the Kingdom of God
Once, having been asked by the Pharisees when the kingdom of God would come, Jesus replied, “The kingdom of God does not come with your careful observation,
NIV.21: nor will people say, ‘Here it is,’ or ‘There it is,’ because the kingdom of God is within you.”
NIV.22: Then he said to his disciples, “The time is coming when you will long to see one of the days of the Son of Man, but you will not see it.
NIV.23: Men will tell you, ‘There he is!’ or ‘Here he is!’ Do not go running off after them.
NIV.24: For the Son of Man in his day will be like the lightning, which flashes and lights up the sky from one end to the other.
NIV.25: But first he must suffer many things and be rejected by this generation.
NIV.26: “Just as it was in the days of Noah, so also will it be in the days of the Son of Man.
NIV.27: People were eating, drinking, marrying and being given in marriage up to the day Noah entered the ark. Then the flood came and destroyed them all.
NIV.28: “It was the same in the days of Lot. People were eating and drinking, buying and selling, planting and building.
NIV.29: But the day Lot left Sodom, fire and sulfur rained down from heaven and destroyed them all.
NIV.30: “It will be just like this on the day the Son of Man is revealed.
NIV.31: On that day no one who is on the roof of his house, with his goods inside, should go down to get them. Likewise, no one in the field should go back for anything.
NIV.33: Whoever tries to keep his life will lose it, and whoever loses his life will preserve it.
NIV.34: I tell you, on that night two people will be in one bed; one will be taken and the other left.
NIV.35: Two women will be grinding grain together; one will be taken and the other left.”
NIV.37:
“Where, Lord?” they asked.
He replied, “Where there is a dead body, there the vultures will gather.”

NRSVCE.1: Jesus said to his disciples, “Occasions for stumbling are bound to come, but woe to anyone by whom they come!
NRSVCE.2: It would be better for you if a millstone were hung around your neck and you were thrown into the sea than for you to cause one of these little ones to stumble.
NRSVCE.3: Be on your guard! If another disciple sins, you must rebuke the offender, and if there is repentance, you must forgive.
NRSVCE.4: And if the same person sins against you seven times a day, and turns back to you seven times and says, ‘I repent,’ you must forgive.”
NRSVCE.5: The apostles said to the Lord, “Increase our faith!”
NRSVCE.6: The Lord replied, “If you had faith the size of a mustard seed, you could say to this mulberry tree, ‘Be uprooted and planted in the sea,’ and it would obey you.
NRSVCE.7: “Who among you would say to your slave who has just come in from plowing or tending sheep in the field, ‘Come here at once and take your place at the table’?
NRSVCE.8: Would you not rather say to him, ‘Prepare supper for me, put on your apron and serve me while I eat and drink; later you may eat and drink’?
NRSVCE.9: Do you thank the slave for doing what was commanded?
NRSVCE.10: So you also, when you have done all that you were ordered to do, say, ‘We are worthless slaves; we have done only what we ought to have done!’”
NRSVCE.11: On the way to Jerusalem Jesus was going through the region between Samaria and Galilee.
NRSVCE.12: As he entered a village, ten lepers approached him. Keeping their distance,
NRSVCE.13: they called out, saying, “Jesus, Master, have mercy on us!”
NRSVCE.14: When he saw them, he said to them, “Go and show yourselves to the priests.” And as they went, they were made clean.
NRSVCE.15: Then one of them, when he saw that he was healed, turned back, praising God with a loud voice.
NRSVCE.16: He prostrated himself at Jesus’ feet and thanked him. And he was a Samaritan.
NRSVCE.17: Then Jesus asked, “Were not ten made clean? But the other nine, where are they?
NRSVCE.18: Was none of them found to return and give praise to God except this foreigner?”
NRSVCE.19: Then he said to him, “Get up and go on your way; your faith has made you well.”
NRSVCE.20: Once Jesus was asked by the Pharisees when the kingdom of God was coming, and he answered, “The kingdom of God is not coming with things that can be observed;
NRSVCE.21: nor will they say, ‘Look, here it is!’ or ‘There it is!’ For, in fact, the kingdom of God is among you.”
NRSVCE.22: Then he said to the disciples, “The days are coming when you will long to see one of the days of the Son of Man, and you will not see it.
NRSVCE.23: They will say to you, ‘Look there!’ or ‘Look here!’ Do not go, do not set off in pursuit.
NRSVCE.24: For as the lightning flashes and lights up the sky from one side to the other, so will the Son of Man be in his day.
NRSVCE.25: But first he must endure much suffering and be rejected by this generation.
NRSVCE.26: Just as it was in the days of Noah, so too it will be in the days of the Son of Man.
NRSVCE.27: They were eating and drinking, and marrying and being given in marriage, until the day Noah entered the ark, and the flood came and destroyed all of them.
NRSVCE.28: Likewise, just as it was in the days of Lot: they were eating and drinking, buying and selling, planting and building,
NRSVCE.29: but on the day that Lot left Sodom, it rained fire and sulfur from heaven and destroyed all of them
NRSVCE.30: — it will be like that on the day that the Son of Man is revealed.
NRSVCE.31: On that day, anyone on the housetop who has belongings in the house must not come down to take them away; and likewise anyone in the field must not turn back.
NRSVCE.33: Those who try to make their life secure will lose it, but those who lose their life will keep it.
NRSVCE.34: I tell you, on that night there will be two in one bed; one will be taken and the other left.
NRSVCE.35: There will be two women grinding meal together; one will be taken and the other left.”
NRSVCE.37: Then they asked him, “Where, Lord?” He said to them, “Where the corpse is, there the vultures will gather.”

NET.1: Jesus said to his disciples, “Stumbling blocks are sure to come, but woe to the one through whom they come!
NET.2: It would be better for him to have a millstone tied around his neck and be thrown into the sea than for him to cause one of these little ones to sin.
NET.3: Watch yourselves! If your brother sins, rebuke him. If he repents, forgive him.
NET.4: Even if he sins against you seven times in a day, and seven times returns to you saying, ‘I repent,’ you must forgive him.”
NET.5: The apostles said to the Lord, “Increase our faith!”
NET.6: So the Lord replied, “If you had faith the size of a mustard seed, you could say to this black mulberry tree, ‘Be pulled out by the roots and planted in the sea,’ and it would obey you.
NET.7: “Would any one of you say to your slave who comes in from the field after plowing or shepherding sheep, ‘Come at once and sit down for a meal’?
NET.8: Won’t the master instead say to him, ‘Get my dinner ready, and make yourself ready to serve me while I eat and drink. Then you may eat and drink’?
NET.9: He won’t thank the slave because he did what he was told, will he?
NET.10: So you too, when you have done everything you were commanded to do, should say, ‘We are slaves undeserving of special praise; we have only done what was our duty.’”
NET.11: Now on the way to Jerusalem, Jesus was passing along between Samaria and Galilee.
NET.12: As he was entering a village, ten men with leprosy met him. They stood at a distance,
NET.13: raised their voices and said, “Jesus, Master, have mercy on us.”
NET.14: When he saw them he said, “Go and show yourselves to the priests.” And as they went along, they were cleansed.
NET.15: Then one of them, when he saw he was healed, turned back, praising God with a loud voice.
NET.16: He fell with his face to the ground at Jesus’ feet and thanked him. (Now he was a Samaritan.)
NET.17: Then Jesus said, “Were not ten cleansed? Where are the other nine?
NET.18: Was no one found to turn back and give praise to God except this foreigner?”
NET.19: Then he said to the man, “Get up and go your way. Your faith has made you well.”
NET.20: Now at one point the Pharisees asked Jesus when the kingdom of God was coming, so he answered, “The kingdom of God is not coming with signs to be observed,
NET.21: nor will they say, ‘Look, here it is!’ or ‘There!’ For indeed, the kingdom of God is in your midst.”
NET.22: Then he said to the disciples, “The days are coming when you will desire to see one of the days of the Son of Man, and you will not see it.
NET.23: Then people will say to you, ‘Look, there he is!’ or ‘Look, here he is!’ Do not go out or chase after them.
NET.24: For just like the lightning flashes and lights up the sky from one side to the other, so will the Son of Man be in his day.
NET.25: But first he must suffer many things and be rejected by this generation.
NET.26: Just as it was in the days of Noah, so too it will be in the days of the Son of Man.
NET.27: People were eating, they were drinking, they were marrying, they were being given in marriage — right up to the day Noah entered the ark. Then the flood came and destroyed them all.
NET.28: Likewise, just as it was in the days of Lot, people were eating, drinking, buying, selling, planting, building;
NET.29: but on the day Lot went out from Sodom, fire and sulfur rained down from heaven and destroyed them all.
NET.30: It will be the same on the day the Son of Man is revealed.
NET.31: On that day, anyone who is on the roof, with his goods in the house, must not come down to take them away, and likewise the person in the field must not turn back.
NET.33: Whoever tries to keep his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life will preserve it.
NET.34: I tell you, in that night there will be two people in one bed; one will be taken and the other left.
NET.35: There will be two women grinding grain together; one will be taken and the other left.”
NET.37: Then the disciples said to him, “Where, Lord?” He replied to them, “Where the dead body is, there the vultures will gather.”
